doc.field: Archibald Lewis Bouton (1872-1941) was a Professor of Rhetoric (1905-1914), Professor of English (1914-1941), and the Dean of New York University's College of Arts and Science (1914-1935). The Archibald Bouton Negatives (circa 1900-1925) consists of photo negatives documenting Bouton's travels within the United Kingdom, France, and the United States.

Archibald Lewis Bouton (1872-1941) was a Professor of Rhetoric (1905-1914), Professor of English (1914-1941), and the Dean of New York University's College of Arts and Science (1914-1935). The Archibald Bouton Negatives (circa 1900-1925) consists of photo negatives documenting Bouton's travels within the United Kingdom, France, and the United States.
Materials in this collection were transferred from the New York University Archives Photograph Collection (PHOTO 00001) in May 2024. The accession number associated with this collection is 2024.045.

Archibald Lewis Bouton was born on September 1, 1872 in Cortland, New York. He received his A.B. from Amherst in 1896 and his M.A. from Columbia in 1900. He first came to New York University in 1898 as an instructor of English. In 1901, he became an assistant professor and then a full professor in 1905. He served as the Professor of Rhetoric between 1905-1914. In 1914, Bouton became head of the English department and served in that position until 1941. Between 1914-1935, he was also the Dean of the College of Arts and Science. Bouton died on April 18, 1941 in Pasadena, California after having been Dean of University College for twenty-one years.

The Archibald Bouton Negatives (circa 1900-1925) consists of photo negatives documenting Bouton's travels within the United Kingdom, France, and the United States. Locations photographed in this collection include, but are not limited to: London, York, Cambridge, Oxford, Warwick, Edinburgh, Versailles, Paris, Verdun, Reims, Pomona, Boston, Pittsburgh, and New York City.

Negatives related to the United States include negatives #1-7, #41-61 and #93-95. Negatives related to the United Kingdom include negatives #8-40 and negatives #62-82. Negatives related to France include negatives #83-92.
